notes: All assumptions annotated with status: sourced (from named primary source), inference (arithmetic from cited facts), or assumption (stated estimate where data is unavailable). High-risk assumptions are flagged.

assumptions.description: Total addressable UK and Ireland farm shops, honesty boxes, and farm gate stalls
assumptions.detail: 3,000 UK farm shops (excluding 400+ self-sufficient large operations from FRA 3,500 total) + 2,000 UK honesty boxes/gate stalls (40% of estimated 5,000 with digital discovery potential) + 700 Ireland/NI (300 serviceable Irish farm shops + 400 serviceable Ireland/NI honesty boxes)

assumptions.description: Claim rate at Month 12: percentage of 953 seeded listings claimed by their owners
assumptions.detail: Saleability critique modelled 10–15% as base case at Month 12 through organic claim plus traffic-triggered outreach. At 15%: 143 claimed listings. Downside: 5% (48 listings). Upper: 25% (238 listings).

assumptions.description: Claim rate at Month 18
assumptions.detail: 238 claimed listings at Month 18. Consistent with progressive claim rate trajectory.

assumptions.description: Claim rate at Month 24
assumptions.detail: 333 claimed listings at Month 24.

assumptions.description: Claim rate at Month 36 (mature state)
assumptions.detail: 477 claimed listings at Month 36. Value-bench.md used 50% as the mature claim rate assumption based on industry directory benchmarks (40–60% for verified-owner-claim workflows).

assumptions.description: Free-to-Bronze conversion rate: base case (percentage of claimed listings converting to paid Bronze)
assumptions.detail: Saleability critique modelled 4–8% as realistic range within 12 months of claim. 6% is the base case mid-point. This is the most sensitive variable in the financial model.

assumptions.description: Free-to-Bronze conversion rate: downside case
assumptions.detail: At 3% conversion: Bronze shops at Month 36 = 28 (vs 57 base); Year 3 revenue ~£26k (vs £60k); break-even moves to Month 60+. Triggers angel seed consideration.

assumptions.description: Free-to-Bronze conversion rate: upside case
assumptions.detail: Value-bench.md best case: 30% Bronze penetration of Year 3 claimed listings = 215 Bronze shops. 10% conversion of claimed is the intermediate upside assumption.

assumptions.description: Bronze-to-Silver progression rate (after 6+ months on Bronze)
assumptions.unit: percent of Bronze shops eligible for Silver upgrade
assumptions.detail: No comparable UK farm shop directory data available. Directional based on SaaS tier progression benchmarks for SMB products where the higher tier provides a clearly differentiated capability (online ordering vs display only). Requires post-launch cohort validation.

assumptions.description: Average Silver GMV per shop per month (early phase, Month 18–30)
assumptions.unit: GBP per Silver shop per month
assumptions.detail: Value-bench.md base case: £1,800/month. Financial model uses £800 in the early phase as the conservative figure (40 orders at £20 average basket). No UK farm shop e-commerce data directly comparable. Shopify-powered farm shops processing £1,500–£3,000/month is the closest proxy. High uncertainty.

assumptions.description: Average Silver GMV per shop per month (Year 3 mature phase)
assumptions.unit: GBP per Silver shop per month
assumptions.detail: Stepping up from £800 early phase to £1,200 by Year 3 as marketplace matures and Farmmap consumer audience grows. Value-bench.md used £1,800 as base case for Year 3 and Year 5 modelling.

assumptions.description: Average Gold GMV per shop per month (early phase)
assumptions.unit: GBP per Gold shop per month
assumptions.detail: Value-bench.md base case: £5,000/month. Financial model uses £2,500 as conservative early-phase figure. Gold eligibility is gated at 3 months on Silver + 50 completed orders, so Gold shops are genuinely active before upgrade. High uncertainty.

assumptions.description: Blended customer acquisition cost (weighted average across all channels)
assumptions.detail: Weighted blend of: FRA channel (warm referral) ~£45, traffic-triggered email ~£85, rep visit ~£190. Channel mix assumption: 30% FRA, 40% email, 30% rep visit = (0.30×45)+(0.40×85)+(0.30×190) = £13.50+£34+£57 = £104.50, rounded to £125 allowing for overhead. Saleability critique estimated £120–£250 blended.

assumptions.description: CAC via FRA warm-referral channel
assumptions.detail: FRA-referred shops convert at ~3× the cold outreach rate; cost is primarily founder time for relationship management plus pro-rata share of FRA agreement costs.

assumptions.description: CAC via traffic-triggered email channel
assumptions.detail: Automated email triggered at 50+ monthly listing sessions; primarily email platform cost (Resend) plus founder follow-up time at 20% conversion of email contacts.

assumptions.description: CAC via personal rep visit (agricultural shows and direct outreach)
assumptions.detail: Saleability critique: personal visits convert at 20–30% but cost £80–£150 per visit including travel and time. At 25% conversion and £120 average visit cost: £120 ÷ 0.25 = £480 fully loaded — but agricultural shows allow multiple contacts per day; show-based CAC modelled at £190 assuming 20 contacts per show day with 25% conversion = £190 per acquired shop.

assumptions.description: Stripe subscription processing fee
assumptions.detail: Stripe UK published pricing 2025. On a £20/month Bronze subscription: fee = £0.88. Net subscription revenue per Bronze shop = £19.12/month.

assumptions.description: Stripe Connect application fee on GMV (marketplace commissions)
assumptions.detail: Stripe Connect Standard payout fee. On £1,000 GMV basket: Farmmap 3% commission = £30; Stripe 0.25% on payout = £2.50. Net commission contribution = £27.50.

assumptions.description: Annual churn rate for Bronze and Silver shops
assumptions.detail: Typical SaaS B2B small business churn rate. At 15% churn: expected Bronze tenure 6.7 years; gross LTV per Bronze shop £1,608. Farm shop owners who see demonstrated value tend to be sticky; 15% is conservative for those with active marketplace GMV.

assumptions.description: Annual churn rate for Gold shops
assumptions.detail: Gold shops are stickier due to dependency on the marketing services bundle (newsletter, social posts, blog post). At 12% churn: expected Gold tenure 8.3 years; gross LTV per Gold shop £34,860.

assumptions.description: Monthly infrastructure cost at Year 2 scale (Supabase Pro + Vercel Pro)
assumptions.detail: Supabase Pro ~£50/month from Q3 Year 2 when active paying subscribers and marketplace transactions warrant it; Vercel ~£20/month from Year 2 at meaningful traffic. Year 1: effectively £0 (Supabase free tier, Vercel free tier).

assumptions.description: Founder salary in Year 1
assumptions.detail: Year 1: bootstrapped; personal capital injection covers living costs. Founder takes no salary from the business in Year 1.

assumptions.description: Founder salary in Year 2
assumptions.detail: Year 2: product generating revenue; founder takes modest salary. This is the primary driver of the Year 2 EBITDA deficit (–£52,928). Without founder salary, Year 2 EBITDA = approximately –£12,928.

assumptions.description: Founder salary in Year 3
assumptions.detail: Year 3 increase reflecting product maturity and revenue growth.

assumptions.description: Personal capital injection at launch
assumptions.detail: Confirmed personal capital from founder. Staged deployment: ~£500 Month 1 (domain, initial legal); ~£600 Months 2–4 (content); ~£2,000 Months 5–8 (legal review); ~£1,900 Months 9–12 (remaining). Year 1 closing balance approximately –£360 on base case.

assumptions.description: Consumer traffic target at Month 12 (monthly active visitors)
assumptions.detail: Intake.md V1 success metric. Realistic range on base case: 40,000–100,000 depending on content programme execution. BigBarn estimated at 30,000–80,000 monthly sessions; 100k requires matching or exceeding BigBarn within 12 months — ambitious but achievable with aggressive SEO.

summary.most_sensitive_assumption: ASM-006 (6% free-to-Bronze conversion rate) — a move to 3% pushes Year 3 revenue from £60k to ~£26k and break-even from Month 42–48 to Month 60+
summary.most_uncertain_assumption: ASM-010 (£800/month Silver GMV) — no directly comparable UK farm shop e-commerce data exists; range could be £400–£3,000/month
